title: DNA Extraction Protocol for Historical Toe Pad Samples from Birds
author: Dave Lutgen
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bm4mk8u6
materials: Buffer ATL (tissue lysis buffer) Qiagen Catalog #19076
QiAmp DNA Micro kit Qiagen Catalog #56304

steps.title: Put toe pad/breast skin tissue (2-3mg) into 1
steps.instruction: Put toe pad/breast skin tissue (2-3mg) into 1.5 mL Safe Lock Tube Eppendorf Catalog #0030120086 (use scalpel tip to transfer).

steps.title: Mince the tissue with a pointed scalpel blade
steps.instruction: Mince the tissue with a pointed scalpel blade. Use a new blade for each sample!

steps.title: To make sure no tissue is...
steps.instruction: To make sure no tissue is left on the blade, pipet 80 µL of Buffer ATL Qiagen Catalog #19076 over the scalpel blade, then remove it and ensure all tissue remains in the digestion.

steps.title: Add 20 µL of Proteinase K,...
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL of Proteinase K, 2mL Qiagen Catalog #19131 and start crushing the tissue with a micropestle. Keep the pestle in the tube.

steps.title: Pipet 100 µL of Buffer ATL...
steps.instruction: Pipet 100 µL of Buffer ATL (tissue lysis buffer) Qiagen Catalog #19076 over the micropestle to ensure all tissue remains in the digestion. Transfer the pestle into the prepared 1.5 mL Safe Lock Tube Eppendorf Catalog #0030120086 for storage.

steps.title: Mix well by vortexing
steps.instruction: Mix well by vortexing. Incubate tubes at 900 rpm, 56 °C, 6 hours . Make sure the timer is on infinity.

steps.title: After 06:00:00 , centrifuge the tubes...
steps.instruction: After 06:00:00 , centrifuge the tubes and add 20 µL of Proteinase K, 2mL Qiagen Catalog #19131 to the remaining tissue. Use the pestle to further crush tissue parts. Pipet a second volume of 180 µL Buffer ATL (tissue lysis buffer) Qiagen Catalog #19076 over the pestle to ensure all tissue remains in the digestion.

steps.title: Preheat AL lysis buffer Qiagen Catalog #19075 at 56 °C
steps.instruction: Preheat AL lysis buffer Qiagen Catalog #19075 at 56 °C . This step prevents the formation of precipitates when AL lysis buffer Qiagen Catalog #19075 is added to the digestion.

steps.title: Prepare a 1:1 dilution of Ultrapure...
steps.instruction: Prepare a 1:1 dilution of Ultrapure Distilled, Nuclease Free Water Contributed by users and AE buffer Qiagen Catalog #19077 . Preheat at 37 °C .

steps.title: Prepare and label 1
steps.instruction: Prepare and label 1.5 mL Safe Lock Tube Eppendorf Catalog #0030120086 for each extraction (2 for 2 elutions)

steps.title: This protocol uses the QiAmp DNA...
steps.instruction: This protocol uses the QiAmp DNA Micro kit Qiagen Catalog #56304 with 2x the amount/volumes of Proteinase K, 2mL Qiagen Catalog #19131 Buffer ATL (tissue lysis buffer) Qiagen Catalog #19076 AL lysis buffer Qiagen Catalog #19075 RNase A Qiagen Catalog #19101 .

steps.title: Prepare a Micropestle Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #BAF199230001 per sample
steps.instruction: Prepare a Micropestle Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #BAF199230001 per sample. Prepare a 1.5 mL Safe Lock Tube Eppendorf Catalog #0030120086 to store the micropestle separately for each sample. Preheat AL lysis buffer Qiagen Catalog #19075 at 56 °C . Adding preheated AL lysis buffer Qiagen Catalog #19075 prevents the formation of potential precipitates.

steps.title: Once all tissue is properly digested,...
steps.instruction: Once all tissue is properly digested, in case there are undigested parts (bones, feathers, etc), centrifuge the lysate at max speed for 00:05:00 , and transfer the lysate into a new tube. Else, continue with the next step.

steps.title: Add 4 µL RNase A Qiagen Catalog #19101
steps.instruction: Add 4 µL RNase A Qiagen Catalog #19101 . Mix by vortexing, and incubate at Room temperature for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Vortex for 00:00:15
steps.instruction: Vortex for 00:00:15 . Add 400 µL of preheated 56 °C AL lysis buffer Qiagen Catalog #19075 . Mix very quickly. A coagulate will form. Dissolve this in the heat block at 900 rpm, 56 °C, 10 minutes

steps.title: Add 400 µL ice-cold ( -20...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL ice-cold ( -20 °C ) Ethanol, absolute 99.8% Contributed by users Catalog #10342652 . Mix very well by vortexing. Incubate at room temperature for 10 min.

steps.title: Apply 650 µL of the lysate to the column
steps.instruction: Apply 650 µL of the lysate to the column. Centrifuge at 8000 rpm, °C, 1 minute . Discard flow-through, dry the collection tube, and place the column back into the collection tube.

steps.title: Apply the rest of the lysate
steps.instruction: Apply the rest of the lysate. Centrifuge at 8000 rpm, °C, 1 minute . Discard flow-through and collection tube.

steps.title: Place the column into a new collection tube
steps.instruction: Place the column into a new collection tube. Add 500 µL Buffer AW1, Wash buffer (1), concentrate Qiagen Catalog #19081 . Centrifuge at 8000 rpm, °C, 1 minute . Discard flow-through and collection tube.

steps.title: Place the column into a new collection tube
steps.instruction: Place the column into a new collection tube. Add 500 µL Buffer AW2, Wash buffer (2), concentrate Qiagen Catalog #19072 . Centrifuge at 8000 rpm, °C, 1 minute . Discard flow-through, dry collection tube, and place column back into it.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 14000 rpm, °C, 2...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 14000 rpm, °C, 2 minutes to dry the membrane. Make sure no ethanol is left anywhere in or on the column. Discard the collection tube.

steps.title: Place the column into a labeled 1
steps.instruction: Place the column into a labeled 1.5 mL Safe Lock Tube Eppendorf Catalog #0030120086 . Apply 20 µL of preheated ( 37 °C ) 1:1 diluted AE buffer Qiagen Catalog #19077 to the column.

steps.title: Put the column into the thermoshaker...
steps.instruction: Put the column into the thermoshaker 0 rpm, 37 °C, 7 minutes Then spin at 8000 rpm, °C, 2 minutes .

steps.title: If you expect high(er) amounts of...
steps.instruction: If you expect high(er) amounts of DNA, repeat steps go to step #25 and go to step #26 with another 20 µL of 1:1 diluted AE buffer Qiagen Catalog #19077 .

steps.title: Turn temperature on the heat block down to 37 °C
steps.instruction: Turn temperature on the heat block down to 37 °C . Preheat 1:1 diluted Buffer AE at 37 °C .

steps.title: Vortex well
steps.instruction: Vortex well. Make sure all pieces of tissue are in solution and continue digestion overnight at 56 °C . Leave to digest 900 rpm, 56 °C, 1 day 16 hours . (Note: Tests with extraction after 24 and 40 hours show that DNA amount doubles within the additional 16 hours).

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: it is possible that with increased digestion time bigger pieces of tissue are becoming visible. This is likely due to increasing hydration/swelling of tissue pieces with prolonged digestion, and exactly what you want)

steps.title: Museum toe pad samples might have...
steps.instruction: Museum toe pad samples might have been treated with chemicals to better conserve them, therefore, we will rince the tissue before starting the digestion

steps.title: Add 1000 µL of Ultrapure Distilled,...
steps.instruction: Add 1000 µL of Ultrapure Distilled, Nuclease Free Water Contributed by users to wash down chemicals on the toe pad. Incubate at 1000 rpm, 56 °C, 1 hour Discard all Ultrapure Distilled, Nuclease Free Water Contributed by users . Make sure no Ultrapure Distilled, Nuclease Free Water Contributed by users is left.

steps.title: Examples of DNA yield per milligram...
steps.instruction: Examples of DNA yield per milligram input tissue for samples of varying age.
